rules.rule: Brokers must be thin wrappers over external resources and must contain no business logic.
Broker interfaces must be named generically — technology must not appear in the interface name.
Storage brokers must use: InsertAsync, SelectAsync, UpdateAsync, DeleteAsync.
API brokers must use: PostAsync, GetAsync, PutAsync, DeleteAsync.
Queue brokers must use: EnqueueAsync, DequeueAsync.
Brokers must accept only primitive types or native models as input.
Brokers must return only the raw external resource response without transformation.
Brokers must retrieve configuration via IConfiguration injected in the constructor.
Brokers must be partial classes when supporting multiple entities.
